question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum concurrent subscriptions per topic limit to subscribe to the same topic from thousands of connections, causing the topicListener to fan out every incoming message to thousands of subscribers simultaneously, multiplying memory and CPU usage and causing a network partition through resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransactionID()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where stored messages contain transactionIdBytes that are valid protobuf but encode a TransactionID with an AccountID containing a very large alias byte array, causing parseTransactionID() to allocate large objects and by subscribing to many such topics, trigger heap exhaustion that partitions the network?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gRPC keep-alive mechanism by opening a subscription and then sending keep-alive pings at maximum frequency, causing the server to spend disproportionate CPU time processing keep-alive frames instead of serving legitimate requests, effectively partitioning the server from the network?
